mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.4% as of Feb 2019 reflects recent rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations upward through increased expected corporate earnings over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150606k as of Feb 2019 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong PAYEMS growth, or correlation between PAYEMS acceleration and forward returns drops below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows declining corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants reprrice equity risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite RSAFS contraction, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below its 6-month trend, signaling supply-side weakness and margin compression that reduces forward equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% and PAYEMS at 150606k as of Feb 2019
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect (UNRATE × PAYEMS) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline combined with falling retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand weakness across residential and consumer sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1.0% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1230k and RSAFS at 504440M as of Jan 2019
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued below 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, the real interest rate is restrictive, signaling monetary tightness that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 253.113 and FEDFUNDS at 2.4% as of Feb 2019
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the real rate signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following sub-2% GDP growth are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
